---
title: "Improve README quick-start for first-time users"
labels: ["good first issue", "docs"]
---

## Context

The README quick-start section assumes the reader already knows what Claude Code and Codex are. A newcomer from a religious-studies or philosophy background may not.

## What to do

- Add a one-sentence explanation of what Claude Code and Codex are (where to get them and what access is required) before the three-mode instructions.
- Add an example of what the output looks like (a short, real excerpt from a council debate) so people can see the value before installing anything.
- Keep it concise — the README is already long. The goal is one extra paragraph, not a tutorial.
- Update **both** the English and 繁體中文 sections.

## Acceptance

- The quick-start still fits on one screen
- A reader with no AI-agent experience can follow it
- No new dependencies or tooling introduced
